title: No, Big Tech Isn’t Hiding $1.65 Trillion of Debt
excerpt: SEC filings show about $430.0 billion of interest-bearing borrowings, not $1.65 trillion of hidden debt.
content_html:
The $1.65 Trillion Total Reconciles

A viral Nikkei article reported that “hidden debt” at five U.S. technology companies had reached $1.65 trillion. That figure sounds implausible beside the companies’ reported debt, but the arithmetic does reconcile to their filings.1

Figure 1. The reconstructed $1.6507 trillion headline is almost evenly split: $821.4 billion of uncommenced leases and $829.2 billion of purchase and construction commitments. Company details and source periods are in C1.

The article did not fabricate the numerator. It appears to have added two broad categories:

  1. lease payments promised under contracts for assets that were not yet available for use
  2. contractual commitments to buy infrastructure, equipment, capacity, energy, inventory, content, and other goods or services

The disclosures are large because AI infrastructure is large, contracts can run for decades, and the table adds nominal payments across those years. Microsoft’s subtotal also mixes reporting dates: its $196.6 billion lease figure is from March 2026, while $142.1 billion of purchase and construction commitments comes from June 2025.

Figure 2. The viral article compares $1.651 trillion of future contracts with $1.350 trillion of total liabilities. The latter includes $430 billion of interest-bearing borrowings; no optional spending plans are included in the headline amount.

Using the same categories in fiscal 2022 produces $206.296 billion. Dividing $1.650676 trillion by that baseline gives 8.0015 times.2 The “eightfold” claim is therefore accurate under the article’s category choice.

The $1.35 Trillion Comparison Reveals the Error

The article says the $1.65 trillion exceeds “roughly $1.35 trillion in debt reflected on their balance sheets.” The five balance sheets do add to $1.3500 trillion, but only if every liability is treated as debt.3 Recognized interest-bearing borrowings total $430.0 billion, less than one-third of the reported liability total.

Total liabilities include far more than bonds and loans. They include accounts payable, accrued compensation, deferred revenue, taxes, recognized lease liabilities, and other obligations arising in normal operations.

Calling total liabilities “debt” makes the comparison look more coherent than it is. The article sets an undiscounted flow of future contractual payments against a current balance-sheet stock, labels both debt, and treats the larger number as evidence of concealment.

Three Different Things Were Collapsed Into “Debt”

The accounting distinction matters because each category has different economics.

Borrowings

Borrowings are funded financing: bonds, notes, term loans, commercial paper, and similar claims. The company has already received cash or another financial asset and owes principal, usually with interest. These obligations sit on the balance sheet.

Uncommenced Leases

An uncommenced lease is a signed contract for the future right to use an asset that is not yet available. A data center may still be under construction, for example.

Under U.S. lease accounting, a lessee generally records a discounted lease liability and an offsetting right-of-use asset when the lease commences, not when the contract is signed.4 That timing explains why future payments for an uncommenced lease can appear in the notes but not yet in the balance-sheet lease liability.

The eventual balance-sheet liability will not ordinarily equal the filing’s nominal payment total. The company discounts the future payments to present value and records an asset on the other side of the entry.

Purchase Commitments

A purchase commitment is an executory contract. The supplier still owes chips, servers, power, capacity, construction, inventory, or services, and the buyer owes payment as delivery occurs.

Those future payments may later become property and equipment, inventory, an operating expense, accounts payable, or a cash outflow. They do not automatically become debt. The SEC has long required companies to disclose material purchase obligations precisely because these contracts can affect future liquidity without being current balance-sheet liabilities.4

The most accurate umbrella label is disclosed but unrecognized future commitments. Some commitments are economically debt-like. The entire $1.65 trillion is not debt.

Contracts Are Not Optional Spending Plans

The $1.65 trillion reconstruction contains no free-standing estimate for voluntary, non-obligated capital spending. It counts amounts the companies described as leases not yet commenced, non-cancelable or unconditional purchase commitments, minimum cancellation fees, take-or-pay contracts, construction commitments, or other contractual obligations.

That distinction closes off a different possible criticism of the viral article. The total is not a sum of management aspirations for future data centers. If a company merely said it hoped to invest another $100 billion, that amount would not belong in this reconstruction. Figure 2 shows the non-obligated portion of the headline total as zero for that reason.

Contractual does not mean every dollar has identical force. Some disclosures include open purchase orders; some contracts set minimum quantities; some allow cancellation after a fee; and some payments depend on delivery or project milestones. The company descriptions establish that an obligation exists, but a credit analysis still has to read the cancellation, substitution, and performance terms.

The figures were taken from public 10-Q and 10-K filings. They appear in notes and liquidity disclosures rather than in the face of the balance sheet because that is where accounting rules place them.

This does not make them easy for every reader to find. The companies use different labels, tables, time horizons, and reporting dates. A balance-sheet-only screen will miss uncommenced leases and executory purchase contracts. That is a disclosure-friction problem, not evidence that the obligations were concealed.

The Enron analogy in the viral article goes much further than the evidence. Enron used entities and transactions to misstate its financial condition. The article identifies no sham transaction, secret vehicle, accounting violation, or false filing at these five companies. Large disclosed commitments can be risky without being an Enron-style fraud.

Scale Relative to Revenue and the Balance Sheet

The aggregate commitment total is roughly equal to one year of revenue at the five companies. Their latest annual filings report $1.6698 trillion of combined revenue and $2.6601 trillion of combined assets.5 The $1.6507 trillion of contracts equals 98.9% of that annual revenue and 62.1% of those assets.

The company-level comparison is more informative than the aggregate.

Figure 3. Contract totals are not directly comparable with revenue or assets, but the scale check shows where the commitments are most material. Revenue and assets are fiscal 2025 figures except Oracle’s fiscal 2026 figures.

Oracle is the outlier. Its $273.3 billion commitment total is more than four times annual revenue and slightly larger than total assets. Meta’s total also exceeds annual revenue and assets. Amazon sits at the other end: its commitments equal 29.3% of annual revenue and 25.7% of assets.

These are scale ratios, not solvency ratios. Revenue is a one-year flow before expenses; assets are a stock measured under accounting rules; and future contracts stretch over different periods. The ratios do not show that Oracle or Meta owes the full amount today. They show why the commitments deserve more analysis than a balance-sheet debt screen provides.

When the Cash Is Due: One Year to Three Decades

The $1.6507 trillion headline total is not due at one time. It combines purchases due within a year with leases that have not started and may continue for decades.6

The companies do not publish one comparable annual schedule. Some disclose exact yearly payments, while others report only a short-term subtotal, commencement window, contract-term range, or undated “thereafter” amount. The available timing disclosures show the range without pretending the filings contain a single annual schedule.

Figure 4. Uncommenced leases begin over several years and may run for as long as 30 years. Lighter bars show outer dates inferred from the latest disclosed start and longest disclosed term, not weighted-average maturities. Amazon does not disclose a final year for its “thereafter” bucket.

The lease tail can extend well beyond the commencement windows. A Meta lease that starts in 2036 and runs for 30 years could continue to 2066. The corresponding outer dates are approximately 2056 for Alphabet, fiscal 2052 for Microsoft, and fiscal 2048 for Oracle. Those dates are outer bounds inferred from the latest possible start and longest stated term, not weighted-average maturity estimates. Amazon does not disclose a final year for its “thereafter” bucket.

An Annualized Illustration, Not a Payment Forecast

The filings do not support an exact annual cash-cost calculation for the complete $1.6507 trillion. Dividing the total evenly across several periods shows its annual scale without claiming to reproduce the contracts.

Figure 5. Spreading $1.6507 trillion evenly produces annualized payments from $165.1 billion over 10 years to $55.0 billion over 30 years. These are arithmetic illustrations, not forecasts of the contracts’ cash flows.

These scenarios divide $1.6507 trillion by the stated number of years and compare the result with $1.6698 trillion of combined annual revenue. They do not account for different commencement dates, uneven payments, discounting, contract amendments, cancellations, or the assets and services received in return. Actual purchase payments are more concentrated in the early years, while much of the uncommenced-lease cash cost begins later.

The timing changes the interpretation. The headline total is a long-dated stream of nominal payments, not a bill payable today. The annual cash burden may still be material, but the public filings do not support calling the full amount current debt or assigning it one precise annual cost.

The Financial Risk Is Still Real

Rejecting the word “debt” does not make the commitments harmless.

Many of the contracts are non-cancelable or take-or-pay. They can reduce financial flexibility if AI demand, utilization, power availability, pricing, or technology lifecycles disappoint. A company may have to keep paying for capacity that earns less than expected. Assets placed in service can also be impaired if their expected cash flows fall.

Some project-finance structures deserve closer analysis. An outside operator may borrow to build a data center while a technology company signs the long lease, purchase agreement, guarantee, or credit backstop that makes the project financeable. The Bank for International Settlements has called this narrower mechanism “shadow borrowing.”7 The operator’s loan may remain outside the customer’s consolidated balance sheet even though the customer’s contract supports it.

That risk should be measured contract by contract:

  • Which payments are fixed, and which depend on usage or delivery?
  • Can the company cancel, substitute capacity, or assume the underlying asset?
  • How long is the commitment, and what discount rate is appropriate?
  • Does a guarantee or backstop create exposure beyond stated rent?
  • What asset, inventory, service, or revenue is expected in return?

Adding nominal payments answers none of those questions. It is a useful scale indicator, not a debt valuation.

Backlog Does Not Cancel the Risk

The article points to approximately $1.45 trillion of cloud and other backlog as an offset. That figure also checks out: Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon reported a combined $1.4646 trillion of remaining performance obligations at March 31, 2026.8

Backlog is not cash or profit. It is contracted future revenue that the companies still have to earn by performing over periods with different durations and conditions. The associated services carry operating and capital costs.

The two large numbers describe opposite sides of an infrastructure buildout: commitments indicate future resource demand, while backlog indicates contracted future customer revenue. Neither can be netted against the other without matching timing, margins, cancellation rights, counterparties, and performance conditions.

A Claim-by-Claim Scorecard

Viral claim Finding
“$1.65 trillion of hidden debt” Misleading. The amount reconciles, but it is neither all debt nor hidden.
It exceeds $1.35 trillion of balance-sheet debt False as labeled. $1.3500 trillion is total liabilities; borrowings are about $430.0 billion.
The total grew eightfold in roughly four years Confirmed under the article’s category set.
Meta is near $420 billion and Oracle is $273.3 billion Confirmed.
Much of the amount will eventually be recorded Partly true. Commenced leases produce discounted lease liabilities; purchases do not all become debt.
Cloud and other backlog is about $1.45 trillion Confirmed. The March 2026 total is $1.4646 trillion, but it is gross future revenue.
The companies depend on debt and equity because investment exceeds earnings Overgeneralized. Financing is rising, but the causal claim does not hold uniformly across the five companies and periods.
The situation resembles Enron Unsupported. The article presents no comparable evidence of concealment or accounting fraud.

Alphabet’s Next Filing Raised the Total to $2.14 Trillion

The viral article was dated July 21, 2026. Alphabet filed its second-quarter 10-Q on July 22.

The new filing disclosed $811.0 billion of purchase and other contractual obligations, $5.8 billion of short-term uncommenced lease payments, and $85.2 billion of long-term uncommenced lease payments.9 Replacing Alphabet’s first-quarter $408.0 billion subtotal with those second-quarter figures, while holding the other four companies fixed, raises the five-company total to about $2.14 trillion.

That update strengthens the warning about scale. It does not change the accounting category.

The viral article found a real signal and gave it the wrong name.

The five companies have made unusually large, long-dated commitments to secure AI infrastructure. Investors should model the fixed payments, the assets and services received in return, the project-finance backstops, and the revenue expected to support them. The notes to the financial statements matter.

But $1.65 trillion was not a hidden pile of loans. It was an aggregation of disclosed, mostly undiscounted future payments under leases and executory contracts. The article then compared that amount with total liabilities, called both sides debt, and implied dollar-for-dollar balance-sheet recognition.

The concise verdict is the useful one: real commitments, real risk, wrong noun.

Disclaimer

This article is independent research based on public filings and accounting materials. It is not investment, legal, tax, or accounting advice and does not consider any reader’s circumstances. The calculations depend on the category definitions and filing dates stated in the appendix and may change as companies file new information. Do your own research and consult a licensed professional before making financial decisions.

Appendix: Sources and Method

Evidence date: July 23, 2026

This appendix supports the material claims in “No, Big Tech Isn’t Hiding $1.65 Trillion of Debt.” It separates reported amounts from calculations and accounting judgments. All dollar amounts are U.S. dollars.

How the Reconstruction Works

The analysis used Finterm’s filing search and extraction tools to locate the relevant disclosures, then checked each amount against the linked primary filing. The reconstruction follows the viral article’s apparent category choice rather than substituting a new definition:

  • uncommenced lease payments
  • purchase, construction, and other contractual commitments

The calculation does not discount future payments. It does not treat cancellable orders as fixed obligations unless a company included them in its disclosed total. It does not add recognized lease liabilities to uncommenced lease payments. Amounts are presented at the precision disclosed by each company.

The companies do not share a fiscal year-end or one uniform reporting format. Most current amounts are from March 31, 2026; Oracle’s are from May 31, 2026. Microsoft’s purchase and construction commitments are from June 30, 2025 because its March 2026 filing updated uncommenced leases without publishing a replacement total for those two categories.

C1. Reconstructing the Headline Total

Claim: The viral article’s $1.65 trillion estimate can be reproduced from public filings.

Status: Supported, subject to the category and date limitations above.

Amounts are billions.

Company Filing period used Uncommenced leases Purchase and construction commitments Total
Meta March 31, 2026 $182.880 $237.670 $420.550
Alphabet March 31, 2026 $75.600 $332.400 $408.000
Microsoft March 31, 2026 leases; June 30, 2025 commitments $196.600 $142.102 $338.702
Oracle May 31, 2026 $260.000 $13.309 $273.309
Amazon March 31, 2026 $106.347 $103.768 $210.115
Total $821.427 $829.249 $1,650.676

Microsoft’s $142.102 billion commitment subtotal consists of $32.149 billion of construction commitments and $109.953 billion of purchase commitments.

Primary Sources

  • Meta Q1 2026 Form 10-Q
  • Alphabet Q1 2026 Form 10-Q
  • Microsoft March 2026 Form 10-Q
  • Microsoft FY2025 Form 10-K
  • Oracle FY2026 Form 10-K
  • Amazon Q1 2026 Form 10-Q

Interpretation Limits

The sum mixes economic categories and reporting dates. Contract lengths also differ. A dollar due next year and a dollar due decades from now receive equal weight in the nominal total. The calculation therefore establishes the scale of stated future payments, not the present value of a debt instrument.

C2. Testing the Balance-Sheet Comparison

Claim: The article’s “roughly $1.35 trillion in debt reflected on their balance sheets” is the sum of total liabilities, not the sum of debt.

Status: Supported.

Amounts are billions. Borrowings use the carrying value of reported interest-bearing debt and exclude lease liabilities.

Company Recognized borrowings Total liabilities
Meta $58.748 $151.569
Alphabet $79.499 $225.173
Microsoft $40.262 $279.861
Oracle $129.541 $218.703
Amazon $121.906 $474.716
Total $429.956 $1,350.022

The total-liabilities amounts come from the same current filings listed under C1. Total liabilities include borrowings but also accounts payable, accrued expenses, deferred revenue, taxes, recognized lease liabilities, and other operating obligations.

The comparison has a second mismatch. The $1.650676 trillion numerator is an undiscounted stream of payments over many years. The $1.350022 trillion denominator is a balance-sheet amount measured at one date under the recognition and measurement rules for each liability class.

C3. Testing the Eightfold Claim

Claim: Applying the same categories to fiscal 2022 yields an eightfold increase.

Status: Supported under the article’s apparent method.

Calculation Amount
Current reconstructed commitments $1,650.676 billion
Comparable fiscal 2022 commitments $206.296 billion
Current divided by fiscal 2022 8.0015 times

The baseline uses each company’s fiscal 2022 annual filing and the same broad categories used in C1. It does not add recognized lease liabilities or balance-sheet debt.

Fiscal 2022 Source Filings

  • Meta FY2022 Form 10-K
  • Alphabet FY2022 Form 10-K
  • Microsoft FY2022 Form 10-K
  • Oracle FY2022 Form 10-K
  • Amazon FY2022 Form 10-K

Interpretation Limits

The ratio is sensitive to contract duration and disclosure scope. A company that replaces a shorter contract with a longer one can report a larger undiscounted commitment even if its annual capacity is unchanged. The five companies also changed their infrastructure mix and disclosure language between 2022 and 2026. The ratio is valid as a same-category reconstruction, not as a measure of an eightfold increase in funded borrowing.

C4. Accounting Treatment

Claim: Uncommenced leases and purchase commitments do not have the same recognition or measurement as debt.

Status: Supported.

Debt

Debt generally reflects funded financing. The borrower received cash or another financial asset and recognizes an obligation to repay principal, usually with interest.

Leases

The Financial Accounting Standards Board’s lease standard requires a lessee to recognize a lease liability and right-of-use asset at the commencement date. The lease liability is based on the present value of lease payments not yet paid. An uncommenced lease has not reached that recognition point because the underlying asset is not yet available for use.

When the lease commences, the accounting entry ordinarily includes both:

  • a discounted lease liability
  • a right-of-use asset, adjusted for items such as prepayments and incentives

The filing’s nominal stream of future rent therefore does not become an equal amount of debt principal on commencement.

Purchase Commitments

Purchase commitments are executory contracts when both sides still owe performance. The supplier owes equipment, power, capacity, construction, inventory, content, or services. The customer owes payment as the contract is performed.

The SEC’s contractual-obligations rules have treated purchase obligations as a distinct disclosure category and explained that the related liability is generally recognized when the counterparty performs. Depending on the transaction, delivery can produce property and equipment, inventory, expense, accounts payable, or another recognized item. It does not mechanically produce debt.

Authoritative Sources

  • FASB ASU 2016-02, Leases
  • SEC release on off-balance-sheet arrangements and contractual obligations

C5. The Risk That Survives the Fact Check

Claim: Some commitments can be economically debt-like and material to credit risk without being accounting debt.

Status: Supported as a credit-analysis judgment; contract terms determine the exposure.

Non-cancelable leases and take-or-pay purchase contracts can create fixed claims on future cash. Their risk depends on duration, cancellation rights, utilization, pricing, asset substitution, counterparty strength, and what the company receives in return.

Project financing adds another layer. An infrastructure operator can borrow against a long-term lease, offtake agreement, guarantee, or credit backstop from a technology company. The operator’s debt may remain outside the customer’s consolidated balance sheet. The customer’s contractual commitment can still be the economic support that makes the financing possible.

The Bank for International Settlements uses “shadow borrowing” for this narrower structure in its analysis of AI infrastructure finance. Moody’s treats uncommenced lease commitments as relevant to credit analysis but applies credit adjustments rather than equating nominal future rent with funded debt.

Sources

  • BIS, “Financing the AI infrastructure boom”
  • Moody’s, hyperscaler lease-commitment analysis

Interpretation Limits

“Debt-like” is an analytical description, not a license to relabel every contractual payment as debt. A credit model should discount the fixed payments, examine termination and backstop terms, and recognize the assets or services received in return.

C6. Testing the Backlog Counterweight

Claim: Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon reported approximately $1.45 trillion of cloud and other backlog at March 31, 2026.

Status: Supported.

Amounts are billions.

Company Remaining performance obligations
Alphabet $467.6
Microsoft $633.0
Amazon $364.0
Total $1,464.6

Primary Sources

  • Alphabet Q1 2026 Form 10-Q
  • Microsoft March 2026 Form 10-Q
  • Amazon Q1 2026 Form 10-Q

Remaining performance obligations are contracted future revenue, including some amounts already recorded as deferred revenue and some to be invoiced later. Recognition depends on performance, contract terms, and customer usage. The totals cover different weighted durations and are gross of the costs required to deliver the services.

The backlog cannot be netted mechanically against purchase and lease commitments. A valid coverage analysis would match counterparties, timing, margins, cancellation rights, and performance conditions.

C7. Alphabet Second-Quarter Update

Claim: Alphabet’s filing one day after the viral article materially increased the same broad commitment total.

Status: Supported.

Alphabet’s July 22, 2026 Form 10-Q disclosed:

Category Amount
Purchase and other contractual obligations $811.0 billion
Short-term payments for leases not yet commenced $5.8 billion
Long-term payments for leases not yet commenced $85.2 billion
Alphabet Q2 subtotal $902.0 billion

Replacing Alphabet’s $408.0 billion first-quarter subtotal in C1 with $902.0 billion gives:

$1,650.676B - $408.0B + $902.0B = $2,144.676B

The updated five-company figure is therefore approximately $2.14 trillion if every other company is held fixed. This is a sensitivity update, not a same-date total for all five companies.

Primary Source

  • Alphabet Q2 2026 Form 10-Q

C8. Scale Relative to Revenue and Assets

Claim: The reconstructed commitments equal 98.9% of the companies’ combined latest annual revenue and 62.1% of their combined latest annual assets.

Status: Supported as a scale comparison, not a liquidity or solvency measure.

Amounts are billions. Revenue and assets come from fiscal 2025 annual filings except Oracle, whose fiscal 2026 annual filing was available. Total liabilities use the latest filing periods in C1.

Company Contracts Annual revenue Total assets Latest total liabilities
Meta $420.550 $200.966 $366.021 $151.569
Alphabet $408.000 $402.836 $595.281 $225.173
Microsoft $338.702 $281.724 $619.003 $279.861
Oracle $273.309 $67.357 $261.759 $218.703
Amazon $210.115 $716.924 $818.042 $474.716
Total $1,650.676 $1,669.807 $2,660.106 $1,350.022

The ratio calculations are:

  • contracts divided by annual revenue: 98.9%
  • contracts divided by total assets: 62.1%
  • contracts divided by latest total liabilities: 122.3%
  • latest total liabilities divided by annual revenue: 80.9%

These ratios mix a multi-year undiscounted payment stream, a one-year revenue flow, and balance-sheet stocks. Using the $430.0 billion recognized-borrowings total from C2, borrowings equal 25.8% of combined annual revenue. They answer a scale question only. They do not measure when cash is due, the margin on associated revenue, the value of assets or services received, or the company’s ability to refinance.

Primary Sources

  • Meta FY2025 Form 10-K
  • Alphabet FY2025 Form 10-K
  • Microsoft FY2025 Form 10-K
  • Oracle FY2026 Form 10-K
  • Amazon FY2025 Form 10-K

C9. Timing and Annualized Payment Scenarios

Claim: The reconstructed commitments span periods ranging from less than one year to approximately 30 years, but the filings do not provide one comparable annual payment schedule for the complete $1.650676 trillion.

Status: Supported.

The relevant timing disclosures are:

Company Uncommenced-lease timing Other commitment timing
Meta $182.880 billion begins from the remainder of 2026 through 2036; terms range from more than one year to 30 years. Of $237.670 billion, $42.250 billion is due in 2026 and $47.650 billion in 2027. The remaining $147.770 billion is due later.
Alphabet $75.600 billion begins during 2026–2031; terms range from 1–25 years. Of $332.400 billion, $138.000 billion is short-term and $194.400 billion is long-term.
Microsoft $196.600 billion begins during fiscal 2026–2031; terms range from 1–21 years. The fiscal 2025 commitment table classifies $130.799 billion as short-term and $11.303 billion as long-term.
Oracle $260.000 billion begins during fiscal 2027–2029; terms generally range from 15–19 years. $1.841 billion is due in fiscal 2027, $1.034 billion in 2028, $1.053 billion in 2029, $0.952 billion in 2030, $0.896 billion in 2031, and $7.533 billion thereafter.
Amazon The filing does not state a final year for $106.347 billion of uncommenced leases. Combined uncommenced-lease and purchase payments are $24.278 billion in the remainder of 2026, $25.442 billion in 2027, $15.697 billion in 2028, $14.623 billion in 2029, $14.622 billion in 2030, and $115.453 billion thereafter.

The possible outer dates for Meta, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Oracle are derived by adding the longest stated term to the latest stated commencement year. They are boundary cases, not expected or weighted-average maturity dates. Amazon reports a “thereafter” bucket without a final year, so the same calculation is not possible.

Annualized Scenario Method

The annualized scenarios use:

annualized nominal payments = $1,650.676 billion ÷ assumed payment years

The revenue comparison uses $1,669.807 billion of combined annual revenue from C8.

Assumed payment years Annualized nominal payments Annualized payments divided by revenue
10 $165.068 billion 9.9%
15 $110.045 billion 6.6%
20 $82.534 billion 4.9%
25 $66.027 billion 4.0%
30 $55.023 billion 3.3%

These are equal-payment illustrations, not forecasts or present-value estimates. The source disclosures show that actual cash payments are uneven. Purchase and construction commitments include substantial near-term amounts, while many uncommenced leases start in later periods. The calculation also does not allocate the payments among companies or adjust for contract amendments, cancellation provisions, discount rates, interest components, or the assets and services obtained.

Primary Sources

  • Meta Q1 2026 Form 10-Q
  • Alphabet Q1 2026 Form 10-Q
  • Microsoft March 2026 Form 10-Q
  • Microsoft FY2025 Form 10-K
  • Oracle FY2026 Form 10-K
  • Amazon Q1 2026 Form 10-Q

Original Claim Reviewed

The fact check addresses the July 21, 2026 Nikkei article supplied for review. The available archived copy preserves the wording used in the analysis:

  • Nikkei article, archived copy

The article’s strongest numerical claims were tested independently against the primary filings rather than accepted from the archived text.

  1. Company figures, source dates, category mapping, and primary filing links: C1.  ↑ 

  2. Fiscal 2022 source filings, category controls, and ratio calculation: C3.  ↑ 

  3. Balance-sheet liabilities, recognized borrowings, and calculation method: C2.  ↑ 

  4. Recognition rules and the debt, lease, and executory-contract distinction: C4.  ↑   ↑ 

  5. Latest annual revenue and assets, latest total liabilities, comparison periods, and ratio calculations: C8.  ↑ 

  6. Company maturity disclosures, explicit annual payment buckets, and annualized scenario calculations: C9.  ↑ 

  7. Credit-analysis treatment, project-finance structures, and the limits of nominal aggregation: C5.  ↑ 

  8. Company-level remaining performance obligations and interpretation: C6.  ↑ 

  9. Alphabet’s July 22 filing and the updated five-company calculation: C7.  ↑

The company discounts the future payments to present value and records an asset on the other side of the entry. Purchase Commitments A purchase commitment is an executory contract. The supplier still owes chips, servers, power, capacity, construction, inventory, or services, and the buyer owes payment as delivery occurs. Those future payments may later become property and equipment, inventory, an operating expense, accounts payable, or a cash outflow. They do not automatically become debt. The SEC has long required companies to disclose material purchase obligations precisely because these contracts can affect future liquidity without being current balance-sheet liabilities.4 The most accurate umbrella label is disclosed but unrecognized future commitments. Some commitments are economically debt-like. The entire $1.65 trillion is not debt. Contracts Are Not Optional Spending Plans The $1.65 trillion reconstruction contains no free-standing estimate for voluntary, non-obligated capital spending. It counts amounts the companies described as leases not yet commenced, non-cancelable or unconditional purchase commitments, minimum cancellation fees, take-or-pay contracts, construction commitments, or other contractual obligations. That distinction closes off a different possible criticism of the viral article. The total is not a sum of management aspirations for future data centers. If a company merely said it hoped to invest another $100 billion, that amount would not belong in this reconstruction. Figure 2 shows the non-obligated portion of the headline total as zero for that reason. Contractual does not mean every dollar has identical force. Some disclosures include open purchase orders; some contracts set minimum quantities; some allow cancellation after a fee; and some payments depend on delivery or project milestones. The company descriptions establish that an obligation exists, but a credit analysis still has to read the cancellation, substitution, and performance terms. The figures were taken from public 10-Q and 10-K filings. They appear in notes and liquidity disclosures rather than in the face of the balance sheet because that is where accounting rules place them. This does not make them easy for every reader to find. The companies use different labels, tables, time horizons, and reporting dates. A balance-sheet-only screen will miss uncommenced leases and executory purchase contracts. That is a disclosure-friction problem, not evidence that the obligations were concealed. The Enron analogy in the viral article goes much further than the evidence. Enron used entities and transactions to misstate its financial condition. The article identifies no sham transaction, secret vehicle, accounting violation, or false filing at these five companies. Large disclosed commitments can be risky without being an Enron-style fraud. Scale Relative to Revenue and the Balance Sheet The aggregate commitment total is roughly equal to one year of revenue at the five companies. Their latest annual filings report $1.6698 trillion of combined revenue and $2.6601 trillion of combined assets.5 The $1.6507 trillion of contracts equals 98.9% of that annual revenue and 62.1% of those assets. The company-level comparison is more informative than the aggregate. Figure 3. Contract totals are not directly comparable with revenue or assets, but the scale check shows where the commitments are most material. Revenue and assets are fiscal 2025 figures except Oracle’s fiscal 2026 figures. Oracle is the outlier. Its $273.3 billion commitment total is more than four times annual revenue and slightly larger than total assets. Meta’s total also exceeds annual revenue and assets. Amazon sits at the other end: its commitments equal 29.3% of annual revenue and 25.7% of assets. These are scale ratios, not solvency ratios. Revenue is a one-year flow before expenses; assets are a stock measured under accounting rules; and future contracts stretch over different periods. The ratios do not show that Oracle or Meta owes the full amount today. They show why the commitments deserve more analysis than a balance-sheet debt screen provides. When the Cash Is Due: One Year to Three Decades The $1.6507 trillion headline total is not due at one time. It combines purchases due within a year with leases that have not started and may continue for decades.6 The companies do not publish one comparable annual schedule. Some disclose exact yearly payments, while others report only a short-term subtotal, commencement window, contract-term range, or undated “thereafter” amount. The available timing disclosures show the range without pretending the filings contain a single annual schedule. Figure 4. Uncommenced leases begin over several years and may run for as long as 30 years. Lighter bars show outer dates inferred from the latest disclosed start and longest disclosed term, not weighted-average maturities. Amazon does not disclose a final year for its “thereafter” bucket. The lease tail can extend well beyond the commencement windows. A Meta lease that starts in 2036 and runs for 30 years could continue to 2066. The corresponding outer dates are approximately 2056 for Alphabet, fiscal 2052 for Microsoft, and fiscal 2048 for Oracle. Those dates are outer bounds inferred from the latest possible start and longest stated term, not weighted-average maturity estimates. Amazon does not disclose a final year for its “thereafter” bucket. An Annualized Illustration, Not a Payment Forecast The filings do not support an exact annual cash-cost calculation for the complete $1.6507 trillion. Dividing the total evenly across several periods shows its annual scale without claiming to reproduce the contracts. Figure 5. Spreading $1.6507 trillion evenly produces annualized payments from $165.1 billion over 10 years to $55.0 billion over 30 years. These are arithmetic illustrations, not forecasts of the contracts’ cash flows. These scenarios divide $1.6507 trillion by the stated number of years and compare the result with $1.6698 trillion of combined annual revenue. They do not account for different commencement dates, uneven payments, discounting, contract amendments, cancellations, or the assets and services received in return. Actual purchase payments are more concentrated in the early years, while much of the uncommenced-lease cash cost begins later. The timing changes the interpretation. The headline total is a long-dated stream of nominal payments, not a bill payable today. The annual cash burden may still be material, but the public filings do not support calling the full amount current debt or assigning it one precise annual cost. The Financial Risk Is Still Real Rejecting the word “debt” does not make the commitments harmless. Many of the contracts are non-cancelable or take-or-pay. They can reduce financial flexibility if AI demand, utilization, power availability, pricing, or technology lifecycles disappoint. A company may have to keep paying for capacity that earns less than expected. Assets placed in service can also be impaired if their expected cash flows fall. Some project-finance structures deserve closer analysis. An outside operator may borrow to build a data center while a technology company signs the long lease, purchase agreement, guarantee, or credit backstop that makes the project financeable. The Bank for International Settlements has called this narrower mechanism “shadow borrowing.”7 The operator’s loan may remain outside the customer’s consolidated balance sheet even though the customer’s contract supports it. That risk should be measured contract by contract: Which payments are fixed, and which depend on usage or delivery? Can the company cancel, substitute capacity, or assume the underlying asset? How long is the commitment, and what discount rate is appropriate? Does a guarantee or backstop create exposure beyond stated rent? What asset, inventory, service, or revenue is expected in return? Adding nominal payments answers none of those questions. It is a useful scale indicator, not a debt valuation. Backlog Does Not Cancel the Risk The article points to approximately $1.45 trillion of cloud and other backlog as an offset. That figure also checks out: Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon reported a combined $1.4646 trillion of remaining performance obligations at March 31, 2026.8 Backlog is not cash or profit. It is contracted future revenue that the companies still have to earn by performing over periods with different durations and conditions. The associated services carry operating and capital costs. The two large numbers describe opposite sides of an infrastructure buildout: commitments indicate future resource demand, while backlog indicates contracted future customer revenue. Neither can be netted against the other without matching timing, margins, cancellation rights, counterparties, and performance conditions. A Claim-by-Claim Scorecard Viral claim Finding “$1.65 trillion of hidden debt” Misleading. The amount reconciles, but it is neither all debt nor hidden. It exceeds $1.35 trillion of balance-sheet debt False as labeled. $1.3500 trillion is total liabilities; borrowings are about $430.0 billion. The total grew eightfold in roughly four years Confirmed under the article’s category set. Meta is near $420 billion and Oracle is $273.3 billion Confirmed. Much of the amount will eventually be recorded Partly true. Commenced leases produce discounted lease liabilities; purchases do not all become debt. Cloud and other backlog is about $1.45 trillion Confirmed. The March 2026 total is $1.4646 trillion, but it is gross future revenue. The companies depend on debt and equity because investment exceeds earnings Overgeneralized. Financing is rising, but the causal claim does not hold uniformly across the five companies and periods. The situation resembles Enron Unsupported. The article presents no comparable evidence of concealment or accounting fraud. Alphabet’s Next Filing Raised the Total to $2.14 Trillion The viral article was dated July 21, 2026. Alphabet filed its second-quarter 10-Q on July 22. The new filing disclosed $811.0 billion of purchase and other contractual obligations, $5.8 billion of short-term uncommenced lease payments, and $85.2 billion of long-term uncommenced lease payments.9 Replacing Alphabet’s first-quarter $408.0 billion subtotal with those second-quarter figures, while holding the other four companies fixed, raises the five-company total to about $2.14 trillion. That update strengthens the warning about scale. It does not change the accounting category. The viral article found a real signal and gave it the wrong name. The five companies have made unusually large, long-dated commitments to secure AI infrastructure. Investors should model the fixed payments, the assets and services received in return, the project-finance backstops, and the revenue expected to support them. The notes to the financial statements matter. But $1.65 trillion was not a hidden pile of loans. It was an aggregation of disclosed, mostly undiscounted future payments under leases and executory contracts. The article then compared that amount with total liabilities, called both sides debt, and implied dollar-for-dollar balance-sheet recognition. The concise verdict is the useful one: real commitments, real risk, wrong noun. Disclaimer This article is independent research based on public filings and accounting materials. It is not investment, legal, tax, or accounting advice and does not consider any reader’s circumstances. The calculations depend on the category definitions and filing dates stated in the appendix and may change as companies file new information. Do your own research and consult a licensed professional before making financial decisions. Appendix: Sources and Method Evidence date: July 23, 2026 This appendix supports the material claims in “No, Big Tech Isn’t Hiding $1.65 Trillion of Debt.” It separates reported amounts from calculations and accounting judgments. All dollar amounts are U.S. dollars. How the Reconstruction Works The analysis used Finterm’s filing search and extraction tools to locate the relevant disclosures, then checked each amount against the linked primary filing. The reconstruction follows the viral article’s apparent category choice rather than substituting a new definition: uncommenced lease payments purchase, construction, and other contractual commitments The calculation does not discount future payments. It does not treat cancellable orders as fixed obligations unless a company included them in its disclosed total. It does not add recognized lease liabilities to uncommenced lease payments. Amounts are presented at the precision disclosed by each company. The companies do not share a fiscal year-end or one uniform reporting format. Most current amounts are from March 31, 2026; Oracle’s are from May 31, 2026. Microsoft’s purchase and construction commitments are from June 30, 2025 because its March 2026 filing updated uncommenced leases without publishing a replacement total for those two categories. C1. Amounts are billions. Contract lengths also differ. A dollar due next year and a dollar due decades from now receive equal weight in the nominal total. The calculation therefore establishes the scale of stated future payments, not the present value of a debt instrument. C2. Testing the Balance-Sheet Comparison Claim: The article’s “roughly $1.35 trillion in debt reflected on their balance sheets” is the sum of total liabilities, not the sum of debt. Status: Supported. Amounts are billions. Borrowings use the carrying value of reported interest-bearing debt and exclude lease liabilities. Company Recognized borrowings Total liabilities Meta $58.748 $151.569 Alphabet $79.499 $225.173 Microsoft $40.262 $279.861 Oracle $129.541 $218.703 Amazon $121.906 $474.716 Total $429.956 $1,350.022 The total-liabilities amounts come from the same current filings listed under C1. Total liabilities include borrowings but also accounts payable, accrued expenses, deferred revenue, taxes, recognized lease liabilities, and other operating obligations. The comparison has a second mismatch. The $1.650676 trillion numerator is an undiscounted stream of payments over many years. The $1.350022 trillion denominator is a balance-sheet amount measured at one date under the recognition and measurement rules for each liability class. C3. Testing the Eightfold Claim Claim: Applying the same categories to fiscal 2022 yields an eightfold increase. Status: Supported under the article’s apparent method. Calculation Amount Current reconstructed commitments $1,650.676 billion Comparable fiscal 2022 commitments $206.296 billion Current divided by fiscal 2022 8.0015 times The baseline uses each company’s fiscal 2022 annual filing and the same broad categories used in C1. It does not add recognized lease liabilities or balance-sheet debt. Fiscal 2022 Source Filings Meta FY2022 Form 10-K Alphabet FY2022 Form 10-K Microsoft FY2022 Form 10-K Oracle FY2022 Form 10-K Amazon FY2022 Form 10-K Interpretation Limits The ratio is sensitive to contract duration and disclosure scope. A company that replaces a shorter contract with a longer one can report a larger undiscounted commitment even if its annual capacity is unchanged. The five companies also changed their infrastructure mix and disclosure language between 2022 and 2026. The ratio is valid as a same-category reconstruction, not as a measure of an eightfold increase in funded borrowing. C4. Accounting Treatment Claim: Uncommenced leases and purchase commitments do not have the same recognition or measurement as debt. Status: Supported. Debt Debt generally reflects funded financing. The borrower received cash or another financial asset and recognizes an obligation to repay principal, usually with interest. Leases The Financial Accounting Standards Board’s lease standard requires a lessee to recognize a lease liability and right-of-use asset at the commencement date. The lease liability is based on the present value of lease payments not yet paid. An uncommenced lease has not reached that recognition point because the underlying asset is not yet available for use. When the lease commences, the accounting entry ordinarily includes both: a discounted lease liability a right-of-use asset, adjusted for items such as prepayments and incentives The filing’s nominal stream of future rent therefore does not become an equal amount of debt principal on commencement. Purchase Commitments Purchase commitments are executory contracts when both sides still owe performance. The supplier owes equipment, power, capacity, construction, inventory, content, or services. The customer owes payment as the contract is performed. The SEC’s contractual-obligations rules have treated purchase obligations as a distinct disclosure category and explained that the related liability is generally recognized when the counterparty performs. Depending on the transaction, delivery can produce property and equipment, inventory, expense, accounts payable, or another recognized item. It does not mechanically produce debt. Authoritative Sources FASB ASU 2016-02, Leases SEC release on off-balance-sheet arrangements and contractual obligations C5. The Risk That Survives the Fact Check Claim: Some commitments can be economically debt-like and material to credit risk without being accounting debt. Status: Supported as a credit-analysis judgment; contract terms determine the exposure. Non-cancelable leases and take-or-pay purchase contracts can create fixed claims on future cash. Their risk depends on duration, cancellation rights, utilization, pricing, asset substitution, counterparty strength, and what the company receives in return. Project financing adds another layer. An infrastructure operator can borrow against a long-term lease, offtake agreement, guarantee, or credit backstop from a technology company. The operator’s debt may remain outside the customer’s consolidated balance sheet. The customer’s contractual commitment can still be the economic support that makes the financing possible. The Bank for International Settlements uses “shadow borrowing” for this narrower structure in its analysis of AI infrastructure finance. Moody’s treats uncommenced lease commitments as relevant to credit analysis but applies credit adjustments rather than equating nominal future rent with funded debt. Sources BIS, “Financing the AI infrastructure boom” Moody’s, hyperscaler lease-commitment analysis Interpretation Limits “Debt-like” is an analytical description, not a license to relabel every contractual payment as debt. A credit model should discount the fixed payments, examine termination and backstop terms, and recognize the assets or services received in return. C6. Testing the Backlog Counterweight Claim: Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon reported approximately $1.45 trillion of cloud and other backlog at March 31, 2026. Status: Supported. Amounts are billions. Company Remaining performance obligations Alphabet $467.6 Microsoft $633.0 Amazon $364.0 Total $1,464.6 Primary Sources Alphabet Q1 2026 Form 10-Q Microsoft March 2026 Form 10-Q Amazon Q1 2026 Form 10-Q Remaining performance obligations are contracted future revenue, including some amounts already recorded as deferred revenue and some to be invoiced later. Recognition depends on performance, contract terms, and customer usage. The totals cover different weighted durations and are gross of the costs required to deliver the services. The backlog cannot be netted mechanically against purchase and lease commitments. A valid coverage analysis would match counterparties, timing, margins, cancellation rights, and performance conditions. C7. blocks.html_en: Total liabilities include far more than bonds and loans. They include accounts payable, accrued compensation, deferred revenue, taxes, recognized lease liabilities, and other obligations arising in normal operations.

sentences.en: Total liabilities include far more than bonds and loans.
sentences.zh: 总负债远远超过债券和贷款。

sentences.en: They include accounts payable, accrued compensation, deferred revenue, taxes, recognized lease liabilities, and other obligations arising in normal operations.
sentences.zh: 它们包括应付账款、应计报酬、递延收入、税金、已确认的租赁负债以及正常运营中产生的其他义务。

blocks.html_en: Calling total liabilities “debt” makes the comparison look more coherent than it is. The article sets an undiscounted flow of future contractual payments against a current balance-sheet stock, labels both debt, and treats the larger number as evidence of concealment.

sentences.en: Calling total liabilities “debt” makes the comparison look more coherent than it is.
sentences.zh: 将总负债称为“债务”使得这种比较看起来比实际情况更加连贯。

sentences.en: The article sets an undiscounted flow of future contractual payments against a current balance-sheet stock, labels both debt, and treats the larger number as evidence of concealment.
sentences.zh: 该文章根据当前资产负债表存量设定了未来合同付款的未贴现流量，对这两种债务进行了标记，并将较大的数字视为隐瞒的证据。

sentences.en: Three Different Things Were Collapsed Into “Debt”
sentences.zh: 三种不同的东西都陷入了“债务”

blocks.html_en: The accounting distinction matters because each category has different economics.

sentences.en: The accounting distinction matters because each category has different economics.
sentences.zh: 会计的区别很重要，因为每个类别都有不同的经济学。

sentences.en: Borrowings
sentences.zh: 借款

blocks.html_en: Borrowings are funded financing: bonds, notes, term loans, commercial paper, and similar claims. The company has already received cash or another financial asset and owes principal, usually with interest. These obligations sit on the balance sheet.

sentences.en: Borrowings are funded financing: bonds, notes, term loans, commercial paper, and similar claims.
sentences.zh: 借款是融资融资：债券、票据、定期贷款、商业票据和类似债权。

sentences.en: The company has already received cash or another financial asset and owes principal, usually with interest.
sentences.zh: 公司已收到现金或其他金融资产，并欠本金，通常还包括利息。

sentences.en: These obligations sit on the balance sheet.
sentences.zh: 这些义务记在资产负债表上。

sentences.en: Uncommenced Leases
sentences.zh: 未开始的租赁

blocks.html_en: An uncommenced lease is a signed contract for the future right to use an asset that is not yet available. A data center may still be under construction, for example.

sentences.en: An uncommenced lease is a signed contract for the future right to use an asset that is not yet available.
sentences.zh: 未开始的租赁是指为未来使用尚未可用的资产的权利而签署的合同。

sentences.en: A data center may still be under construction, for example.
sentences.zh: 例如，数据中心可能仍在建设中。

blocks.html_en: Under U.S. lease accounting, a lessee generally records a discounted lease liability and an offsetting right-of-use asset when the lease commences, not when the contract is signed.4 That timing explains why future payments for an uncommenced lease can appear in the notes but not yet in the balance-sheet lease liability.

sentences.en: Under U.S. lease accounting, a lessee generally records a discounted lease liability and an offsetting right-of-use asset when the lease commences, not when the contract is signed.4 That timing explains why future payments for an uncommenced lease can appear in the notes but not yet in the balance-sheet lease liability.
sentences.zh: 根据美国租赁会计，承租人通常在租赁开始时（而不是签订合同时）记录贴现租赁负债和抵消使用权资产。4这一时间安排解释了为什么未开始租赁的未来付款可以出现在附注中，但尚未出现在资产负债表租赁负债中。

blocks.html_en: The eventual balance-sheet liability will not ordinarily equal the filing’s nominal payment total. The company discounts the future payments to present value and records an asset on the other side of the entry.

sentences.en: The eventual balance-sheet liability will not ordinarily equal the filing’s nominal payment total.
sentences.zh: 最终的资产负债表负债通常不等于备案的名义付款总额。

sentences.en: The company discounts the future payments to present value and records an asset on the other side of the entry.
sentences.zh: 公司将未来付款折现为现值，并在条目的另一侧记录资产。

sentences.en: Purchase Commitments
sentences.zh: 采购承诺

blocks.html_en: A purchase commitment is an executory contract. The supplier still owes chips, servers, power, capacity, construction, inventory, or services, and the buyer owes payment as delivery occurs.

sentences.en: A purchase commitment is an executory contract.
sentences.zh: 购买承诺是一份可执行的合同。

sentences.en: The supplier still owes chips, servers, power, capacity, construction, inventory, or services, and the buyer owes payment as delivery occurs.
sentences.zh: 供应商仍然欠芯片、服务器、电力、容量、建设、库存或服务，而买方在交货时欠付款。

blocks.html_en: Those future payments may later become property and equipment, inventory, an operating expense, accounts payable, or a cash outflow. They do not automatically become debt. The SEC has long required companies to disclose material purchase obligations precisely because these contracts can affect future liquidity without being current balance-sheet liabilities.4

sentences.en: Those future payments may later become property and equipment, inventory, an operating expense, accounts payable, or a cash outflow.
sentences.zh: 这些未来的付款可能会变成财产和设备、库存、运营费用、应付账款或现金流出。

sentences.en: They do not automatically become debt.
sentences.zh: 它们不会自动成为债务。

sentences.en: The SEC has long required companies to disclose material purchase obligations precisely because these contracts can affect future liquidity without being current balance-sheet liabilities.4
sentences.zh: SEC 长期以来一直要求公司披露重大采购义务，因为这些合同可能会影响未来的流动性，但不会成为当前的资产负债表负债。 4

blocks.html_en: The most accurate umbrella label is disclosed but unrecognized future commitments. Some commitments are economically debt-like. The entire $1.65 trillion is not debt.

sentences.en: The most accurate umbrella label is disclosed but unrecognized future commitments.
sentences.zh: 最准确的保护伞标签是公开但未认可的未来承诺。

sentences.en: Some commitments are economically debt-like.
sentences.zh: 有些承诺在经济上类似于债务。

sentences.en: The entire $1.65 trillion is not debt.
sentences.zh: 全部 1.65 万亿美元都不是债务。

sentences.en: Contracts Are Not Optional Spending Plans
sentences.zh: 合同不是可选的支出计划

blocks.html_en: The $1.65 trillion reconstruction contains no free-standing estimate for voluntary, non-obligated capital spending. It counts amounts the companies described as leases not yet commenced, non-cancelable or unconditional purchase commitments, minimum cancellation fees, take-or-pay contracts, construction commitments, or other contractual obligations.

sentences.en: The $1.65 trillion reconstruction contains no free-standing estimate for voluntary, non-obligated capital spending.
sentences.zh: 1.65 万亿美元的重建不包含对自愿、非义务资本支出的独立估计。

sentences.en: It counts amounts the companies described as leases not yet commenced, non-cancelable or unconditional purchase commitments, minimum cancellation fees, take-or-pay contracts, construction commitments, or other contractual obligations.
sentences.zh: 它计算了公司所描述的尚未开始的租赁、不可取消或无条件的购买承诺、最低取消费用、照付不议合同、建设承诺或其他合同义务的金额。

blocks.html_en: That distinction closes off a different possible criticism of the viral article. The total is not a sum of management aspirations for future data centers. If a company merely said it hoped to invest another $100 billion, that amount would not belong in this reconstruction. Figure 2 shows the non-obligated portion of the headline total as zero for that reason.

sentences.en: That distinction closes off a different possible criticism of the viral article.
sentences.zh: 这种区别消除了对这篇病毒式文章的不同可能的批评。

sentences.en: The total is not a sum of management aspirations for future data centers.
sentences.zh: 这个总数并不是管理层对未来数据中心期望的总和。

sentences.en: If a company merely said it hoped to invest another $100 billion, that amount would not belong in this reconstruction.
sentences.zh: 如果一家公司只是表示希望再投资1000亿美元，那么这笔资金就不属于这次重建。

sentences.en: Figure 2 shows the non-obligated portion of the headline total as zero for that reason.
sentences.zh: 因此，图 2 显示标题总额的非义务部分为零。

blocks.html_en: Contractual does not mean every dollar has identical force. Some disclosures include open purchase orders; some contracts set minimum quantities; some allow cancellation after a fee; and some payments depend on delivery or project milestones. The company descriptions establish that an obligation exists, but a credit analysis still has to read the cancellation, substitution, and performance terms.

sentences.en: Contractual does not mean every dollar has identical force.
sentences.zh: 契约并不意味着每一美元都具有相同的效力。

sentences.en: Some disclosures include open purchase orders; some contracts set minimum quantities; some allow cancellation after a fee; and some payments depend on delivery or project milestones.
sentences.zh: 一些披露包括未结采购订单；有些合同规定了最低数量；有些允许付费后取消；有些付款取决于交付或项目里程碑。

sentences.en: The company descriptions establish that an obligation exists, but a credit analysis still has to read the cancellation, substitution, and performance terms.
sentences.zh: 公司描述表明存在义务，但信用分析仍需阅读取消、替代和履约条款。

blocks.html_en: The figures were taken from public 10-Q and 10-K filings. They appear in notes and liquidity disclosures rather than in the face of the balance sheet because that is where accounting rules place them.

sentences.en: The figures were taken from public 10-Q and 10-K filings.
sentences.zh: 这些数据取自公开的 10-Q 和 10-K 文件。

sentences.en: They appear in notes and liquidity disclosures rather than in the face of the balance sheet because that is where accounting rules place them.
sentences.zh: 它们出现在票据和流动性披露中，而不是出现在资产负债表上，因为这是会计规则放置它们的地方。

blocks.html_en: This does not make them easy for every reader to find. The companies use different labels, tables, time horizons, and reporting dates. A balance-sheet-only screen will miss uncommenced leases and executory purchase contracts. That is a disclosure-friction problem, not evidence that the obligations were concealed.

sentences.en: This does not make them easy for every reader to find.
sentences.zh: 这并不能让每个读者都能轻松找到它们。

sentences.en: The companies use different labels, tables, time horizons, and reporting dates.
sentences.zh: 这些公司使用不同的标签、表格、时间范围和报告日期。

sentences.en: A balance-sheet-only screen will miss uncommenced leases and executory purchase contracts.
sentences.zh: 仅显示资产负债表的屏幕将错过未开始的租赁和正在执行的购买合同。

sentences.en: That is a disclosure-friction problem, not evidence that the obligations were concealed.
sentences.zh: 这是一个披露摩擦问题，而不是隐瞒义务的证据。

blocks.html_en: The Enron analogy in the viral article goes much further than the evidence. Enron used entities and transactions to misstate its financial condition. The article identifies no sham transaction, secret vehicle, accounting violation, or false filing at these five companies. Large disclosed commitments can be risky without being an Enron-style fraud.

sentences.en: The Enron analogy in the viral article goes much further than the evidence.
sentences.zh: 这篇病毒式传播的文章中与安然公司的类比比证据更进一步。

sentences.en: Enron used entities and transactions to misstate its financial condition.
sentences.zh: 安然利用实体和交易谎报其财务状况。

sentences.en: The article identifies no sham transaction, secret vehicle, accounting violation, or false filing at these five companies.
sentences.zh: 文章指出，这五家公司不存在任何虚假交易、秘密工具、会计违规或虚假申报。

sentences.en: Large disclosed commitments can be risky without being an Enron-style fraud.
sentences.zh: 大量披露的承诺即使不构成安然式欺诈，也可能存在风险。

blocks.html_en: Scale Relative to Revenue and the Balance Sheet

sentences.en: Scale Relative to Revenue and the Balance Sheet
sentences.zh: 相对于收入和资产负债表的规模

blocks.html_en: The aggregate commitment total is roughly equal to one year of revenue at the five companies. Their latest annual filings report $1.6698 trillion of combined revenue and $2.6601 trillion of combined assets.5 The $1.6507 trillion of contracts equals 98.9% of that annual revenue and 62.1% of those assets.

sentences.en: The aggregate commitment total is roughly equal to one year of revenue at the five companies.
sentences.zh: 承诺总额大致相当于五家公司一年的收入。

sentences.en: Their latest annual filings report $1.6698 trillion of combined revenue and $2.6601 trillion of combined assets.5 The $1.6507 trillion of contracts equals 98.9% of that annual revenue and 62.1% of those assets.
sentences.zh: 他们最新的年度报告显示，总收入为 1.6698 万亿美元，总资产为 2.6601 万亿美元。5 1.6507 万亿美元的合同相当于年收入的 98.9% 和资产的 62.1%。

blocks.html_en: The company-level comparison is more informative than the aggregate.

sentences.en: The company-level comparison is more informative than the aggregate.
sentences.zh: 公司层面的比较比总体的信息更丰富。

blocks.html_en: Figure 3. Contract totals are not directly comparable with revenue or assets, but the scale check shows where the commitments are most material. Revenue and assets are fiscal 2025 figures except Oracle’s fiscal 2026 figures.

sentences.en: Figure 3.
Contract totals are not directly comparable with revenue or assets, but the scale check shows where the commitments are most material.
sentences.zh: 合同总额不能直接与收入或资产进行比较，但规模检查显示了哪些承诺是最重要的。

sentences.en: Revenue and assets are fiscal 2025 figures except Oracle’s fiscal 2026 figures.
sentences.zh: 除 Oracle 2026 财年数据外，收入和资产均为 2025 财年数据。

blocks.html_en: Oracle is the outlier. Its $273.3 billion commitment total is more than four times annual revenue and slightly larger than total assets. Meta’s total also exceeds annual revenue and assets. Amazon sits at the other end: its commitments equal 29.3% of annual revenue and 25.7% of assets.

sentences.en: Oracle is the outlier.
sentences.zh: 甲骨文是个例外。

sentences.en: Its $273.3 billion commitment total is more than four times annual revenue and slightly larger than total assets.
sentences.zh: 其承诺总额达 2,733 亿美元，是年收入的四倍多，略高于总资产。

sentences.en: Meta’s total also exceeds annual revenue and assets.
sentences.zh: Meta 的总额也超过了年收入和资产。

sentences.en: Amazon sits at the other end: its commitments equal 29.3% of annual revenue and 25.7% of assets.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊则处于另一端：其承诺相当于年收入的 29.3% 和资产的 25.7%。

blocks.html_en: These are scale ratios, not solvency ratios. Revenue is a one-year flow before expenses; assets are a stock measured under accounting rules; and future contracts stretch over different periods. The ratios do not show that Oracle or Meta owes the full amount today. They show why the commitments deserve more analysis than a balance-sheet debt screen provides.

sentences.en: These are scale ratios, not solvency ratios.
sentences.zh: 这些是规模比率，而不是偿付能力比率。

sentences.en: Revenue is a one-year flow before expenses; assets are a stock measured under accounting rules; and future contracts stretch over different periods.
sentences.zh: 收入是扣除费用前的一年流量；资产是根据会计规则计量的存量；未来的合同涵盖不同的时期。

sentences.en: The ratios do not show that Oracle or Meta owes the full amount today.
sentences.zh: 这些比率并未显示 Oracle 或 Meta 今天欠下全部金额。

sentences.en: They show why the commitments deserve more analysis than a balance-sheet debt screen provides.
sentences.zh: 它们说明了为什么这些承诺值得比资产负债表债务筛选提供更多分析。

blocks.html_en: When the Cash Is Due: One Year to Three Decades

sentences.en: When the Cash Is Due: One Year to Three Decades
sentences.zh: 现金到期时间：一年到三年

blocks.html_en: The $1.6507 trillion headline total is not due at one time. It combines purchases due within a year with leases that have not started and may continue for decades.6

sentences.en: The $1.6507 trillion headline total is not due at one time.
sentences.zh: 1.6507 万亿美元的总体总额并非一次性到期。

sentences.en: It combines purchases due within a year with leases that have not started and may continue for decades.6
sentences.zh: 它将一年内到期的购买与尚未开始且可能持续数十年的租赁结合起来。6

blocks.html_en: The companies do not publish one comparable annual schedule. Some disclose exact yearly payments, while others report only a short-term subtotal, commencement window, contract-term range, or undated “thereafter” amount. The available timing disclosures show the range without pretending the filings contain a single annual schedule.

sentences.en: The companies do not publish one comparable annual schedule.
sentences.zh: 这些公司没有公布可比较的年度时间表。

sentences.en: Some disclose exact yearly payments, while others report only a short-term subtotal, commencement window, contract-term range, or undated “thereafter” amount.
sentences.zh: 有些披露了确切的年度付款，而另一些则仅报告了短期小计、启动窗口、合同期限范围或未注明日期的“此后”金额。

sentences.en: The available timing disclosures show the range without pretending the filings contain a single annual schedule.
sentences.zh: 可用的时间披露显示了范围，但没有假装文件包含单一的年度时间表。

blocks.html_en: Figure 4. Uncommenced leases begin over several years and may run for as long as 30 years. Lighter bars show outer dates inferred from the latest disclosed start and longest disclosed term, not weighted-average maturities. Amazon does not disclose a final year for its “thereafter” bucket.

sentences.en: Figure 4.
Uncommenced leases begin over several years and may run for as long as 30 years.
sentences.zh: 未开始的租赁期长达数年，最长可达 30 年。

sentences.en: Lighter bars show outer dates inferred from the latest disclosed start and longest disclosed term, not weighted-average maturities.
sentences.zh: 较浅的条形显示根据最新披露的开始日期和最长披露期限推断的外部日期，而不是加权平均到期日。

sentences.en: Amazon does not disclose a final year for its “thereafter” bucket.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊没有透露其“此后”桶的最后一年。

blocks.html_en: The lease tail can extend well beyond the commencement windows. A Meta lease that starts in 2036 and runs for 30 years could continue to 2066. The corresponding outer dates are approximately 2056 for Alphabet, fiscal 2052 for Microsoft, and fiscal 2048 for Oracle. Those dates are outer bounds inferred from the latest possible start and longest stated term, not weighted-average maturity estimates. Amazon does not disclose a final year for its “thereafter” bucket.

sentences.en: The lease tail can extend well beyond the commencement windows.
sentences.zh: 租赁尾部可以远远超出启动窗口。

sentences.en: A Meta lease that starts in 2036 and runs for 30 years could continue to 2066.
sentences.zh: Meta 租赁从 2036 年开始，为期 30 年，可能会持续到 2066 年。

sentences.en: The corresponding outer dates are approximately 2056 for Alphabet, fiscal 2052 for Microsoft, and fiscal 2048 for Oracle.
sentences.zh: 对应的外部日期对于 Alphabet 大约为 2056 年，对于 Microsoft 大约为 2052 财年，对于 Oracle 大约为 2048 财年。

sentences.en: Those dates are outer bounds inferred from the latest possible start and longest stated term, not weighted-average maturity estimates.
sentences.zh: 这些日期是根据最晚可能开始时间和最长规定期限推断的外部界限，而不是加权平均期限估计。

sentences.en: Amazon does not disclose a final year for its “thereafter” bucket.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊没有透露其“此后”桶的最后一年。

blocks.html_en: An Annualized Illustration, Not a Payment Forecast

sentences.en: An Annualized Illustration, Not a Payment Forecast
sentences.zh: 年化说明，而非付款预测

blocks.html_en: The filings do not support an exact annual cash-cost calculation for the complete $1.6507 trillion. Dividing the total evenly across several periods shows its annual scale without claiming to reproduce the contracts.

sentences.en: The filings do not support an exact annual cash-cost calculation for the complete $1.6507 trillion.
sentences.zh: 文件不支持 1.6507 万亿美元的准确年度现金成本计算。

sentences.en: Dividing the total evenly across several periods shows its annual scale without claiming to reproduce the contracts.
sentences.zh: 将总数平均分配到几个时期即可显示其年度规模，而无需声称复制合同。

blocks.html_en: Figure 5. Spreading $1.6507 trillion evenly produces annualized payments from $165.1 billion over 10 years to $55.0 billion over 30 years. These are arithmetic illustrations, not forecasts of the contracts’ cash flows.

sentences.en: Figure 5.
Spreading $1.6507 trillion evenly produces annualized payments from $165.1 billion over 10 years to $55.0 billion over 30 years.
sentences.zh: 平均分配 1.6507 万亿美元，年化支付额从 10 年内的 1651 亿美元增加到 30 年内的 550 亿美元。

sentences.en: These are arithmetic illustrations, not forecasts of the contracts’ cash flows.
sentences.zh: 这些是算术说明，而不是合同现金流量的预测。

blocks.html_en: These scenarios divide $1.6507 trillion by the stated number of years and compare the result with $1.6698 trillion of combined annual revenue. They do not account for different commencement dates, uneven payments, discounting, contract amendments, cancellations, or the assets and services received in return. Actual purchase payments are more concentrated in the early years, while much of the uncommenced-lease cash cost begins later.

sentences.en: These scenarios divide $1.6507 trillion by the stated number of years and compare the result with $1.6698 trillion of combined annual revenue.
sentences.zh: 这些情景将 1.6507 万亿美元除以规定的年数，并将结果与​​ 1.6698 万亿美元的年度总收入进行比较。

sentences.en: They do not account for different commencement dates, uneven payments, discounting, contract amendments, cancellations, or the assets and services received in return.
sentences.zh: 它们没有考虑不同的开始日期、不均匀付款、折扣、合同修改、取消或作为回报收到的资产和服务。

sentences.en: Actual purchase payments are more concentrated in the early years, while much of the uncommenced-lease cash cost begins later.
sentences.zh: 实际购买付款更多集中在早期，而大部分未开始的租赁现金成本则在后期开始。

blocks.html_en: The timing changes the interpretation. The headline total is a long-dated stream of nominal payments, not a bill payable today. The annual cash burden may still be material, but the public filings do not support calling the full amount current debt or assigning it one precise annual cost.

sentences.en: The timing changes the interpretation.
sentences.zh: 时间改变了解释。

sentences.en: The headline total is a long-dated stream of nominal payments, not a bill payable today.
sentences.zh: 总体总额是长期的名义付款流，而不是今天的应付帐单。

sentences.en: The annual cash burden may still be material, but the public filings do not support calling the full amount current debt or assigning it one precise annual cost.
sentences.zh: 年度现金负担可能仍然很大，但公开文件不支持将其称为全额流动债务或将其分配为一项精确的年度成本。

blocks.html_en: The Financial Risk Is Still Real

sentences.en: The Financial Risk Is Still Real
sentences.zh: 金融风险仍然存在

blocks.html_en: Rejecting the word “debt” does not make the commitments harmless.

sentences.en: Rejecting the word “debt” does not make the commitments harmless.
sentences.zh: 拒绝“债务”一词并不意味着承诺就无害。

blocks.html_en: Many of the contracts are non-cancelable or take-or-pay. They can reduce financial flexibility if AI demand, utilization, power availability, pricing, or technology lifecycles disappoint. A company may have to keep paying for capacity that earns less than expected. Assets placed in service can also be impaired if their expected cash flows fall.

The Bank for International Settlements has called this narrower mechanism “shadow borrowing.”7 The operator’s loan may remain outside the customer’s consolidated balance sheet even though the customer’s contract supports it.

blocks.html_en: The Financial Accounting Standards Board’s lease standard requires a lessee to recognize a lease liability and right-of-use asset at the commencement date. The lease liability is based on the present value of lease payments not yet paid. An uncommenced lease has not reached that recognition point because the underlying asset is not yet available for use.

sentences.zh: 租赁负债基于尚未支付的租赁付款额的现值。

sentences.en: An uncommenced lease has not reached that recognition point because the underlying asset is not yet available for use.
sentences.zh: 未开始的租赁尚未达到该确认点，因为相关资产尚未可供使用。

blocks.html_en: Purchase commitments are executory contracts when both sides still owe performance. The supplier owes equipment, power, capacity, construction, inventory, content, or services. The customer owes payment as the contract is performed.

sentences.zh: 当双方尚有履约义务时，采购承诺即为可执行合同。

blocks.html_en: The SEC’s contractual-obligations rules have treated purchase obligations as a distinct disclosure category and explained that the related liability is generally recognized when the counterparty performs. Depending on the transaction, delivery can produce property and equipment, inventory, expense, accounts payable, or another recognized item. It does not mechanically produce debt.

sentences.en: The SEC’s contractual-obligations rules have treated purchase obligations as a distinct disclosure category and explained that the related liability is generally recognized when the counterparty performs.

sentences.en: Depending on the transaction, delivery can produce property and equipment, inventory, expense, accounts payable, or another recognized item.

sentences.en: It does not mechanically produce debt.
sentences.zh: 它不会机械地产生债务。

content_html_zh:
1.65 万亿美元的总金额对账

《日经新闻》一篇文章报道称，五家美国科技公司的“隐性债务”已达 1.65 万亿美元。 与公司报告的债务相比，这个数字听起来难以置信，但计算结果确实与他们的文件相符。1

图 1. 重建后的 1.6507 万亿美元总体支出几乎平分秋色：8214 亿美元的未开始租赁和 8292 亿美元的采购和建设承诺。 公司详细信息和来源期间位于 C1 中。

这篇文章没有捏造分子。 它似乎增加了两大类：

  1. 根据合同承诺支付尚未可使用的资产的租赁付款
  2. 购买基础设施、设备、产能、能源、库存、内容和其他商品或服务的合同承诺

披露的内容很大，因为人工智能基础设施规模庞大，合同可以运行数十年，而且该表增加了这些年的名义付款。 微软的小计还混合了报告日期：其 1,966 亿美元的租赁数据来自 2026 年 3 月，而 1,421 亿美元的采购和建设承诺来自 2025 年 6 月。

图 2. 这篇病毒式传播的文章将 1.651 万亿美元的未来合同与 1.350 万亿美元的总负债进行了比较。 后者包括 4,300 亿美元的有息借款；标题金额中不包含可选支出计划。

使用相同的类别，2022 财年将产生 2062.96 亿美元的收入。 将 1.650676 万亿美元除以该基线得出 8.0015 倍。2 因此，根据文章的类别选择，“八倍”说法是准确的。

1.35 万亿美元的比较揭示了错误

文章称，1.65 万亿美元超过了“资产负债表上反映的大约 1.35 万亿美元债务”。 五张资产负债表的总和确实达到了 1.3500 万亿美元，但前提是每项负债都被视为债务。3 已确认的带息借款总额为 4,300 亿美元，不到报告负债总额的三分之一。

总负债远远超过债券和贷款。 它们包括应付账款、应计报酬、递延收入、税金、已确认的租赁负债以及正常运营中产生的其他义务。

将总负债称为“债务”使得这种比较看起来比实际情况更加连贯。 该文章根据当前资产负债表存量设定了未来合同付款的未贴现流量，对这两种债务进行了标记，并将较大的数字视为隐瞒的证据。

三种不同的东西都陷入了“债务”

会计的区别很重要，因为每个类别都有不同的经济学。

借款

借款是融资融资：债券、票据、定期贷款、商业票据和类似债权。 公司已收到现金或其他金融资产，并欠本金，通常还包括利息。 这些义务记在资产负债表上。

未开始的租赁

未开始的租赁是指为未来使用尚未可用的资产的权利而签署的合同。 例如，数据中心可能仍在建设中。

根据美国租赁会计，承租人通常在租赁开始时（而不是签订合同时）记录贴现租赁负债和抵消使用权资产。4这一时间安排解释了为什么未开始租赁的未来付款可以出现在附注中，但尚未出现在资产负债表租赁负债中。

最终的资产负债表负债通常不等于备案的名义付款总额。 公司将未来付款折现为现值，并在条目的另一侧记录资产。

采购承诺

购买承诺是一份可执行的合同。 供应商仍然欠芯片、服务器、电力、容量、建设、库存或服务，而买方在交货时欠付款。

这些未来的付款可能会变成财产和设备、库存、运营费用、应付账款或现金流出。 它们不会自动成为债务。 SEC 长期以来一直要求公司披露重大采购义务，因为这些合同可能会影响未来的流动性，但不会成为当前的资产负债表负债。 4

最准确的保护伞标签是公开但未认可的未来承诺。 有些承诺在经济上类似于债务。 全部 1.65 万亿美元都不是债务。

合同不是可选的支出计划

1.65 万亿美元的重建不包含对自愿、非义务资本支出的独立估计。 它计算了公司所描述的尚未开始的租赁、不可取消或无条件的购买承诺、最低取消费用、照付不议合同、建设承诺或其他合同义务的金额。

这种区别消除了对这篇病毒式文章的不同可能的批评。 这个总数并不是管理层对未来数据中心期望的总和。 如果一家公司只是表示希望再投资1000亿美元，那么这笔资金就不属于这次重建。 因此，图 2 显示标题总额的非义务部分为零。

契约并不意味着每一美元都具有相同的效力。 一些披露包括未结采购订单；有些合同规定了最低数量；有些允许付费后取消；有些付款取决于交付或项目里程碑。 公司描述表明存在义务，但信用分析仍需阅读取消、替代和履约条款。

这些数据取自公开的 10-Q 和 10-K 文件。 它们出现在票据和流动性披露中，而不是出现在资产负债表上，因为这是会计规则放置它们的地方。

这并不能让每个读者都能轻松找到它们。 这些公司使用不同的标签、表格、时间范围和报告日期。 仅显示资产负债表的屏幕将错过未开始的租赁和正在执行的购买合同。 这是一个披露摩擦问题，而不是隐瞒义务的证据。

这篇病毒式传播的文章中与安然公司的类比比证据更进一步。 安然利用实体和交易谎报其财务状况。 文章指出，这五家公司不存在任何虚假交易、秘密工具、会计违规或虚假申报。 大量披露的承诺即使不构成安然式欺诈，也可能存在风险。

相对于收入和资产负债表的规模

承诺总额大致相当于五家公司一年的收入。 他们最新的年度报告显示，总收入为 1.6698 万亿美元，总资产为 2.6601 万亿美元。5 1.6507 万亿美元的合同相当于年收入的 98.9% 和资产的 62.1%。

公司层面的比较比总体的信息更丰富。

图 3. 合同总额不能直接与收入或资产进行比较，但规模检查显示了哪些承诺是最重要的。 除 Oracle 2026 财年数据外，收入和资产均为 2025 财年数据。

甲骨文是个例外。 其承诺总额达 2,733 亿美元，是年收入的四倍多，略高于总资产。 Meta 的总额也超过了年收入和资产。 亚马逊则处于另一端：其承诺相当于年收入的 29.3% 和资产的 25.7%。

这些是规模比率，而不是偿付能力比率。 收入是扣除费用前的一年流量；资产是根据会计规则计量的存量；未来的合同涵盖不同的时期。 这些比率并未显示 Oracle 或 Meta 今天欠下全部金额。 它们说明了为什么这些承诺值得比资产负债表债务筛选提供更多分析。

现金到期时间：一年到三年

1.6507 万亿美元的总体总额并非一次性到期。 它将一年内到期的购买与尚未开始且可能持续数十年的租赁结合起来。6

这些公司没有公布可比较的年度时间表。 有些披露了确切的年度付款，而另一些则仅报告了短期小计、启动窗口、合同期限范围或未注明日期的“此后”金额。 可用的时间披露显示了范围，但没有假装文件包含单一的年度时间表。

图 4. 未开始的租赁期长达数年，最长可达 30 年。 较浅的条形显示根据最新披露的开始日期和最长披露期限推断的外部日期，而不是加权平均到期日。 亚马逊没有透露其“此后”桶的最后一年。

租赁尾部可以远远超出启动窗口。 Meta 租赁从 2036 年开始，为期 30 年，可能会持续到 2066 年。 对应的外部日期对于 Alphabet 大约为 2056 年，对于 Microsoft 大约为 2052 财年，对于 Oracle 大约为 2048 财年。 这些日期是根据最晚可能开始时间和最长规定期限推断的外部界限，而不是加权平均期限估计。 亚马逊没有透露其“此后”桶的最后一年。

年化说明，而非付款预测

文件不支持 1.6507 万亿美元的准确年度现金成本计算。 将总数平均分配到几个时期即可显示其年度规模，而无需声称复制合同。

图 5. 平均分配 1.6507 万亿美元，年化支付额从 10 年内的 1651 亿美元增加到 30 年内的 550 亿美元。 这些是算术说明，而不是合同现金流量的预测。

这些情景将 1.6507 万亿美元除以规定的年数，并将结果与​​ 1.6698 万亿美元的年度总收入进行比较。 它们没有考虑不同的开始日期、不均匀付款、折扣、合同修改、取消或作为回报收到的资产和服务。 实际购买付款更多集中在早期，而大部分未开始的租赁现金成本则在后期开始。

时间改变了解释。 总体总额是长期的名义付款流，而不是今天的应付帐单。 年度现金负担可能仍然很大，但公开文件不支持将其称为全额流动债务或将其分配为一项精确的年度成本。

金融风险仍然存在

拒绝“债务”一词并不意味着承诺就无害。

许多合同是不可取消的或照付不议的。 如果人工智能的需求、利用率、电力可用性、定价或技术生命周期令人失望，他们可能会降低财务灵活性。 公司可能不得不继续为收入低于预期的产能付费。 如果投入使用的资产的预期现金流量下降，也可能会受到损害。

一些项目融资结构值得仔细分析。 外部运营商可以借款建设数据中心，而科技公司则签署长期租赁、购买协议、担保或信贷支持，使项目具有融资能力。 国际清算银行将这种范围较窄的机制称为“影子借款”。7即使客户的合同支持，运营商的贷款也可能保留在客户的合并资产负债表之外。

该风险应逐个合同来衡量：

  • 哪些付款是固定的，哪些取决于使用情况或交付情况？
  • 公司可以取消、替代产能或承担标的资产吗？
  • 承诺期限是多长，什么折扣率合适？
  • 担保或担保是否会造成超出规定租金的风险？
  • 预期获得哪些资产、库存、服务或收入回报？

添加象征性付款并不能回答这些问题。 这是一个有用的规模指标，而不是债务估值。

积压并不能消除风险

文章指出，大约 1.45 万亿美元的云和其他积压订单可以作为抵消。 该数字还得到验证：Alphabet、微软和亚马逊报告称，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，剩余履约义务总计 1.4646 万亿美元。8

积压订单不是现金或利润。 它是公司仍必须通过在不同期限和条件的时期内执行任务来赚取的合同未来收入。 相关服务承担运营和资本成本。

这两个大数字描述了基础设施建设的相反方面：承诺表明未来的资源需求，而积压表明合同中的未来客户收入。 如果时间、保证金、取消权、交易对手和履约条件不匹配，则两者都无法相互抵消。

病毒式传播 寻找
“1.65万亿美元的隐性债务” 误导。 金额对账，但既不是全部债务，也不是隐藏的。
资产负债表债务超过 1.35 万亿美元 如标签所示为假。负债总额为 13,500 万亿美元；借款约为4300亿美元。
总数在大约四年内增长了八倍 在文章的类别集下确认。

云和其他积压订单约为 1.45 万亿美元 确认的。 2026 年 3 月的总收入为 14646 亿美元，但这是未来的总收入。
这些公司依赖债务和股权，因为投资超过收益 过于笼统。 融资正在增加，但因果关系在这五家公司和时期内并不统一。
情况类似于安然公司 不支持。 该文章没有提供类似的隐瞒或会计欺诈证据。

Alphabet 的下一份文件使总资产增至 2.14 万亿美元

这篇病毒式传播的文章日期为 2026 年 7 月 21 日。 Alphabet 于 7 月 22 日提交了第二季度 10-Q 财报。

新文件披露了 8110 亿美元的购买和其他合同义务、58 亿美元的短期未开始租赁付款和 852 亿美元的长期未开始租赁付款。 9 用第二季度的数据取代 Alphabet 第一季度 4080 亿美元小计，同时保持其他四家公司不变，五家公司的总金额达到约 2.14 万亿美元。

该更新加强了有关规模的警告。 它不会改变会计类别。

这篇病毒式传播的文章发现了一个真实的信号，但却给它起了错误的名字。

这五家公司为确保人工智能基础设施的安全做出了异常大规模、长期的承诺。 投资者应该对固定付款、作为回报收到的资产和服务、项目融资支持以及支持它们的预期收入进行建模。 财务报表附注很重要。

但 1.65 万亿美元并不是一笔隐藏的贷款。 它是根据租赁和执行合同披露的、大部分未贴现的未来付款的汇总。 然后，文章将这一金额与总负债（称为双方债务）进行了比较，并暗示了资产负债表上的美元兑美元确认。

简洁的结论是有用的：真正的承诺，真正的风险，错误的名词。

免责声明

本文是基于公开文件和会计材料的独立研究。 它不是投资、法律、税务或会计建议，也没有考虑任何读者的情况。 计算取决于附录中规定的类别定义和提交日期，并且可能会随着公司提交新信息而变化。 在做出财务决定之前，请自行研究并咨询有执照的专业人士。

C4。 会计处理

索赔：未开始的租赁和购买承诺不具有与债务相同的确认或计量。

状态：支持。

Debt

债务通常反映融资情况。 借款人收到现金或其他金融资产，并确认有义务偿还本金，通常还包括利息。

租赁

财务会计准则委员会的租赁准则要求承租人在租赁开始日确认租赁负债和使用权资产。 租赁负债基于尚未支付的租赁付款额的现值。 未开始的租赁尚未达到该确认点，因为相关资产尚未可供使用。

租赁开始时，会计分录通常包括：

  • 贴现租赁负债
  • 使用权资产，根据预付款和激励等项目进行调整

因此，该文件中未来租金的名义流在启动时不会成为等额的债务本金。

采购承诺

当双方尚有履约义务时，采购承诺即为可执行合同。 供应商欠有设备、电力、产能、建设、库存、内容或服务。 合同履行时客户欠款。

美国证券交易委员会的合同义务规则将购买义务视为一个独特的披露类别，并解释说，相关责任通常在交易对手履约时予以确认。 根据交易的不同，交货可以产生财产和设备、库存、费用、应付账款或其他已识别的项目。 它不会机械地产生债务。

C5。 经过事实核查的风险

索赔：某些承诺在经济上可能类似于债务，并且对信用风险具有重大意义，但不属于会计债务。

状态：支持作为信用分析判断；合同条款决定风险。

不可取消的租赁和照付不议的购买合同可以对未来现金产生固定债权。 他们的风险取决于期限、取消权、利用率、定价、资产替代、交易对手实力以及公司收到的回报。

项目融资又增加了一层。 基础设施运营商可以通过技术公司的长期租赁、承购协议、担保或信贷支持进行借款。 运营商的债务可能仍在客户的合并资产负债表之外。 客户的合同承诺仍然可以成为使融资成为可能的经济支持。

国际清算银行在分析人工智能基础设施融资时，将“影子借款”用于这种较窄的结构。 穆迪将未开始的租赁承诺视为与信用分析相关，但会进行信用调整，而不是将名义未来租金与融资债务等同起来。

“类似债务”是一种分析性描述，并不是将每笔合同付款重新标记为债务的许可。 信贷模型应对固定付款进行贴现，检查终止和支持条款，并确认收到的资产或服务作为回报。

积压订单不能机械地与购买和租赁承诺相抵销。 有效的覆盖范围分析将匹配交易对手、时间安排、保证金、取消权和履约条件。
